content.text: No openssl-toolbox pod exists, so I'll create a helper to generate certs. CockroachDB's own binary generates valid certs easily. Let me create a cert-generation pod using the cockroach image.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' | kubectl apply -f -
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  name: openssl-toolbox
  namespace: cockroachdb
spec:
  containers:
  - name: toolbox
    image: cockroachdb/cockroach:v24.1.0
    imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
    command: ["/bin/bash","-c","sleep 3600"]
  restartPolicy: Never
EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb wait --for=condition=Ready pod/openssl-toolbox --timeout=120s 2>&1

kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- bash -c '
set -e
cd /tmp
rm -rf certs my-safe-directory
mkdir -p certs my-safe-directory
# CA
./cockroach cert create-ca --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
# Node cert with all SANs
./cockroach cert create-node \
  localhost 127.0.0.1 \
  crdb-cluster \
  crdb-cluster-public \
  crdb-cluster.cockroachdb \
  crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb \
  crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
  crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
  "*.crdb-cluster" \
  "*.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb" \
  "*.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local" \
  --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
# Client root cert
./cockroach cert create-client root --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
ls -la certs
'
input.description: Generate CA, node, and client certs

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- bash -c '
set -e
cd /tmp
rm -rf certs my-safe-directory
mkdir -p certs my-safe-directory
CR=/cockroach/cockroach
$CR cert create-ca --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
$CR cert create-node \
  localhost 127.0.0.1 \
  crdb-cluster crdb-cluster-public \
  crdb-cluster.cockroachdb crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb \
  crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
  crdb-cluster-public.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local \
  "*.crdb-cluster" "*.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb" \
  "*.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local" \
  --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
$CR cert create-client root --certs-dir=certs --ca-key=my-safe-directory/ca.key
ls -la certs
'

content.text: Certs generated. Now copy them out and create Kubernetes secrets.

input.command:
cd /tmp && rm -rf crdb-certs && mkdir crdb-certs
for f in ca.crt node.crt node.key client.root.crt client.root.key; do
  kubectl -n cockroachdb exec openssl-toolbox -- cat /tmp/certs/$f > /tmp/crdb-certs/$f
done
ls -la /tmp/crdb-certs
echo "=== create node secret ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-node-certs \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/ca.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.key 2>&1
echo "=== create client secret ==="
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-client-certs \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/ca.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.key 2>&1
input.description: Copy certs out and create secrets

content.text: Now patch the StatefulSet to secure mode with the cert volume mounted.

input.command:
cat <<'EOF' > /tmp/sts-patch.yaml
spec:
  template:
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: db
        command:
        - /bin/bash
        - -c
        - exec /cockroach/cockroach start --logtostderr=INFO --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs --advertise-host=$(POD_NAME).crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local --http-addr=0.0.0.0:8080 --port=26257 --cache=25% --max-sql-memory=25% --join=crdb-cluster-0.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-1.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257,crdb-cluster-2.crdb-cluster.cockroachdb.svc.cluster.local:26257
        volumeMounts:
        - mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-data
          name: datadir
        - mountPath: /cockroach/cockroach-certs
          name: certs
      volumes:
      - name: certs
        secret:
          secretName: crdb-node-certs
          defaultMode: 256
EOF
kubectl -n cockroachdb patch statefulset crdb-cluster --type=strategic --patch-file /tmp/sts-patch.yaml 2>&1

content.text: Force all pods to restart together (secure nodes can't talk to insecure ones, so a one-by-one rollout would stall).

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb delete pod crdb-cluster-0 crdb-cluster-1 crdb-cluster-2 --grace-period=10 2>&1
echo "=== waiting ==="
sleep 5
kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods 2>&1

input.command:
for i in $(seq 1 24); do
  ready=$(kubectl -n cockroachdb get pods -l app.kubernetes.io/instance=crdb-cluster --no-headers 2>/dev/null | awk '{print $2}' | grep -c '1/1')
  echo "ready=$ready"
  if [ "$ready" = "3" ]; then break; fi
  sleep 5
done

content.text: Let me verify access. The secure `cockroach sql` as root needs client.root certs in the certs-dir — let me test first.

input.command:
echo "=== insecure (should be rejected) ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --insecure -e "SELECT 1;" 2>&1 | head -20
echo "=== secure ==="; kubectl -n cockroachdb exec crdb-cluster-0 -- ./cockroach sql --certs-dir=/cockroach/cockroach-certs -e "SELECT 1;" 2>&1 | head -20

content.text: Insecure is rejected. Secure falls back to password because client.root cert isn't in the dir. Add client certs to the mounted secret.

input.command:
kubectl -n cockroachdb create secret generic crdb-node-certs \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/ca.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/node.key \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.crt \
  --from-file=/tmp/crdb-certs/client.root.key \
  --dry-run=client -o yaml | kubectl apply -f - 2>&1
input.description: Update node secret to include client root certs
